title: alpinia rosea
intro: Alpinia rosea, also known as Indian Galangal or Blue Ginger, is a tropical plant native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, belonging to the ginger family (Zingiberaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Alpinia rosea include reducing nausea and vomiting, alleviating digestive issues, and providing relief from pain and fever. Alpinia rosea can be prepared as a tea, oil, or extract, and is also used as a spice in some recipes, showcasing its versatility in herbal preparations.
